task: Build a Python CLI tool called "log_analyzer" that reads a log file (sample.log), parses it, and outputs a summary report; create multiple files (main.py as entrypoint, parser.py to parse log lines, analyzer.py to compute stats, utils.py for helpers); the log format is "[TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] message" (e.g., "[2025-01-01 10:00:00] [ERROR] Something failed"); features: count total logs, count logs by level (INFO, WARNING, ERROR), print top 3 most frequent messages, and handle malformed lines gracefully without crashing; use only Python standard library; the program must run with "python main.py sample.log"; if sample.log does not exist, create one with at least 10 mixed entries; do not explain—directly build, run, debug, and iterate until it works; success is when running the command prints a clean summary with no crashes and handles bad input safely.

plan.description: CLI tool that reads a log file, parses it, and outputs a summary report
plan.architecture: Python CLI with multiple files for parsing, analyzing, and utilities

files.purpose: parse log lines and extract relevant information
compute statistics from parsed log data
helper functions for file I/O and error handling

build_steps: create sample.log with at least 10 mixed entries

subtasks.name: parse log lines
subtasks.description: read log file, parse each line, and extract relevant information

subtasks.name: compute statistics
subtasks.description: use parsed data to compute total logs, logs by level, top 3 most frequent messages, and handle malformed lines gracefully
